channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Tech salaries 2026: what corrected, what grew
item.description: In 2026 Nairobi tech salaries split 35-50% between generalist CRUD roles and high-leverage platform roles — compare exact numbers and learn which skills moved up and which stayed flat.

item.title: JSON mode doesn’t validate outputs
item.description: Cut AI parsing failures 36% with JSON Schema validation — why JSON mode fails, how to validate outputs, and the 3-step pipeline that works in 2026.

item.title: GIL-free Python 3.13: double web server speed
item.description: Cut API response time 40% with Python 3.13 free-threaded GIL removal — benchmarks, thread safety traps, and the one command to try tonight

item.title: AI interviews break systems: 5 real mistakes in 2026
item.description: The symptom is consistent: the candidate submits a Go 1.22 service, a Node 20 LTS frontend, and a Terraform 1.6 stack, but the build fails with this exact…

item.title: LLM security mistakes in 2026: OWASP Top 10 fixes
item.description: Cut LLM security risks 65% with OWASP Top 10 fixes — 3 concrete numbers, code samples, and the 2026 edition of the OWASP LLM Top 10.

item.title: USSD apps: why African fintech still needs them in 2026
item.description: USSD apps still drive 34% of new signups in African fintech in 2026. Build a production-grade USSD interface with Redis 7.2, FastAPI, and Twilio in 450 lines. Latency, cost, and hiring data included.

item.title: AI codebase? Guardrails over guardrails
item.description: Cut false-positive alerts 30% with SonarQube 10.4 vs GitHub Advanced Security — connection pool crashes, XPath rules, and the $81 monthly price tag most teams miss.

item.title: False positives killed our CI scans — here’s how we
item.description: Cut security scan noise 78% with dynamic suppression — semgrep 1.71, Trivy 0.53, and a Redis 7.2 cache that expires false positives in 30 days.

item.title: Run APIs in 50 spots: what breaks first
item.description: Cut API latency 60% by running in 50 edge locations — cache stampedes, clock drift, and write fan-out mistakes most teams make in 2026.

item.title: Webhooks 2026: retries, idempotency, and the 99.95%
item.description: Cut webhook failures 95% with Redis Streams, Lambda SnapStart, and idempotency keys — p99 latency 780 ms, cost $1.01 per 1,000 events in 2026

item.title: 11 patterns for eventual consistency that don’t wake
item.description: Cut duplicate processing 68% with idempotent messages — the 11 patterns that keep systems up when parts are out of sync, including code, cost, and the outage that taught me the hard way.

item.title: Vibe coding: MVP gem, prod albatross
item.description: Cut long-term debugging time 70% by swapping vibe coding for typed, tested, linted code from day one — with concrete benchmarks, error rates, and a 5-minute action step.

item.title: Signals: 5 state hacks that actually work
item.description: Cut React render time 99% with Signals — benchmarks, code, and the one mistake teams keep making

item.title: Fine-tune vs prompt: 40% cheaper with 3% accuracy loss
item.description: By late 2025, every vendor’s README shows a 1-shot accuracy number on the MMLU-Pro 2026 benchmark that looks impressive: 87.3%.

item.title: Audit logs: append-only DB vs sidecar queues
item.description: Cut audit log latency 4× with PostgreSQL 15 logical decoding vs Kafka 3.7 sidecar queues — concrete benchmarks, costs, and the compliance trick most teams miss

item.title: AI agents break when users cheat
item.description: Cut prompt injection 94% with a 3-layer firewall — token sanitizer, context filter, and model guardrail. Latency +25%, cost +17%, real production data from 50k daily prompts in 2026.

item.title: AI SaaS build: solo vs agent team in 2026
item.description: Spend $3k vs $700 to build a solo SaaS in 2026? Benchmarks from two real products show why targeted AI beats full agent teams — with concrete numbers, pitfalls, and the 30-minute audit you can run today.

item.title: Paddle over Stripe? 2026’s real winner
item.description: Save 20% on EU VAT compliance with Paddle — real fees, benchmarks, and the mistake most teams make when choosing Stripe vs Paddle vs Lemon Squeezy in 2026.

item.title: Branching databases locally: the Neon gotcha nobody
item.description: Cut local DB setup time 70% with Neon and PlanetScale branches — the missing query parameter that wastes hours and the 3 fixes that actually work in 2026.

item.title: RAG isn’t enough in 2026
item.description: Cut hallucinations 75% with hybrid search + reranking — benchmarked on 1,084 questions with Python 3.11, costing $0.00012 extra per query on AWS Bedrock 2026.
